messages.content: ⁠⁠I've read that DEW21 usually gives a 4-week notice before stopping supply for non-payment. Are there any specific scenarios in the 'General Terms' where they are legally allowed to suspend my electricity immediately without any prior warning?

According to the General Terms provided, DEW21 is legally allowed to suspend the supply of electricity immediately without any prior warning under certain circumstances related to significant and unauthorized use of electricity. Specifically, if a customer has used electricity by bypassing, influencing (manipulating), or before the installation of metering devices ("electricity theft"), DEW21 can immediately suspend the supply and have the connection interrupted to prevent further unauthorized energy consumption. Additionally, in cases of repeated offenses, DEW21 is entitled to terminate the contract without observing any notice period for important reasons.

Sources: [Allgemeine_Lieferbedingungen_Strom.pdf_en.txt]
